_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-8 Adult Fever (Initial 10/24/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. Applies to adult patients > 14 years of age.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-8: Adult Fever — MDHHS Approved 10/24/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: Intended to assist EMS providers in reducing fever in the adult patient (> 14 years of age) prior to arrival to the emergency department. Fever is defined as a temperature of 100.4°F (38°C) or greater. Emergency management involves assessment to determine if any associated problems require emergency treatment.

sections.title: Adult Fever Treatment
points: Assess for sepsis and treat according to Sepsis-Treatment Protocol if applicable
Obtain baseline temperature and document method used
Facilitate passive cooling by removing excess clothing and blankets

If patient has not received an antipyretic in the last four (4) hours, is alert, and is able to swallow: administer antipyretic according to MCA selection — must select at least one: ibuprofen 400 mg PO (do NOT use in pregnant patients) or acetaminophen 650 mg PO
Dosing questions should be directed to online medical control

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select at least one antipyretic option for adult fever management
options: Ibuprofen 400 mg PO (do NOT use in pregnant patients)

key_drugs.name: Ibuprofen 400 mg PO
key_drugs.indication: Adult fever (temp ≥ 100.4°F / 38°C) — patient alert, able to swallow, no antipyretic in past 4 hours
key_drugs.contraindications: Pregnant patients — DO NOT USE; known allergy to NSAIDs; active GI bleeding; renal impairment (use with caution)
key_drugs.side_effects: GI upset, renal effects with chronic use
key_drugs.notes: Do NOT administer to pregnant patients. Contact Medical Control for dosing questions.

key_drugs.name: Acetaminophen 650 mg PO
key_drugs.indication: Adult fever (temp ≥ 100.4°F / 38°C) — patient alert, able to swallow, no antipyretic in past 4 hours
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity; severe hepatic impairment; confirm patient has not taken acetaminophen within 4 hours
key_drugs.side_effects: Hepatotoxicity with overdose; generally well-tolerated at therapeutic doses
key_drugs.notes: Safe in pregnancy. Contact Medical Control for dosing questions.

deterioration_flags: Sepsis criteria met (temp abnormality + HR > 90, RR < 10 or > 20, SBP < 90, or hypoperfusion) — refer to Sepsis-Treatment Protocol
Patient unable to swallow or altered mental status — do NOT administer oral medications; passive cooling only; contact Medical Control
Temperature > 40°C (104°F) with altered mental status — consider heat stroke; refer to Heat Emergencies-Treatment Protocol
scope_notes: Temperature assessment, passive cooling, sepsis screening: all levels
Antipyretic administration (ibuprofen or acetaminophen PO): per MCA selection — may be all levels
IV access and fluid administration: Specialist skill (per Sepsis protocol if criteria met)
